name: Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop
name_original: Abenójar, Saceruela y Luciana — circular por el Río Guadiana
description: This difficult 72.0-mile road cycling route near the Río Guadiana offers diverse landscapes and 3337 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by open plains, rolling hills, and river valleys. The region features agricultural areas interspersed with natural features such as the Valle de Alcudia and the Tabla de la Yedra. Road cyclists can expect routes with varying elevation profiles, ranging from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop, a 84.7 miles (136.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views across the Campo de Calatrava and through the Valle de Alcudia. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop, a difficult 72.0 miles (116.0 km) path. This route navigates through rural villages and varied terrain with notable elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena, a 65.0 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through the area around Los Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava is defined by open countryside, riverine landscapes, and rolling hills. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Pozuelos De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes range from moderate to difficult, traversing the region's open plains, rolling hills, and river valleys.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: The road cycling routes around Los Pozuelos De Calatrava are generally geared towards cyclists with some experience. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy,' 9 of the 13 routes are rated as 'moderate,' which may be suitable for beginners looking for a bit of a challenge.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251860322/campo-de-calatrava-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant distances and elevation gains. This particular route covers 84.7 miles (136.3 km) with over 1100 meters of ascent, providing extensive views across the landscape.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: Yes, several routes offer substantial distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086215201/saceruela-y-los-pozuelos-de-calatrava-desde-piedrabuena-circular-por-luciana-y-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena – circular por Luciana y Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt; route is one of the longest, spanning over 81 miles (131 km) with considerable elevation changes, perfect for a full day's ride.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251026703/by-the-pozuelos-de-calatrava-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-circular-by-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 65-mile (104.7 km) loop through varied terrain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several natural points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4017346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorge Narrows of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; also presents a dramatic landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while road cycling?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, you can discover historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sheep Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3762521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geldres Mill&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical sites that add character to the routes. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6654195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a point of interest.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: The terrain is characterized by a mix of open plains, gently rolling hills, and river valleys. You'll find agricultural areas interspersed with natural features, offering varied scenery and elevation profiles, from moderate climbs to more demanding ascents.

Q: Which route is recommended for experiencing rural villages and varied terrain?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This difficult 72-mile (116 km) route navigates through several rural villages and offers a diverse landscape with notable elevation changes, providing a comprehensive experience of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable if cycling during those months.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Valle de Alcudia?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251860322/campo-de-calatrava-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase this area. It offers extensive views across the Campo de Calatrava and directly through the Valle de Alcudia, providing a deep immersion in the regional landscape.

Q: How popular is road cycling in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava among the komoot community?
A: Road cycling in Los Pozuelos De Calatrava is quite popular, with over 100 komoot users having explored the region's varied terrain. The routes are well-regarded for their scenic beauty and the diverse challenges they offer.
tips: The town of is one of the oldest towns in the area, at the service of pilgrims on the road to Andalusia. Today it sits on the C-424 highway that connects Puertollano with Almadén. You can stop to rest in the tree-lined Plaza de San Juan Bautista and admire the façade of the parish church, built with local stone and baked red brick.

Saceruela is a small rural town next to the CM-415 road. Rest in the Nuestra Señora de las Cruces Parish located in the Plaza de la Constitución. The town is one of the starting points of the historic route of the Camino de Guadalupe de Levante that connects the Iberian Peninsula with the town of Guadalupe in Cáceres.

In the western part of the province and the historic Campo de Calatrava, between the Canalizos and San Antón mountain ranges and the Guadiana valley, with very rugged relief and evocative landscapes, is the pleasant town of Luciana.

It houses the mouths of two tributaries of the Guadiana River: the Bullaque in the northeast, very close to the town, and the Tirteafuera in the southwest, in Tablacaldera. Water, always very present, gives uniqueness and beauty to the environment.
Views of the Cañada from the CR-4125.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and covers approximately 116 kilometers. With an estimated duration of around 5 hours and 22 minutes, it's a challenging ride best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking spots aren't detailed, you can generally find parking in the towns along the route, such as Abenójar, Saceruela, or Luciana. Starting from any of these points allows you to complete the circular journey.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for a traditional road bike?
faqs.answer: While designated for racebikes, the broader Guadiana River region can feature varied terrain. Some sections might be paved, but others could include forest tracks, wide dirt tracks, or even rocky paths. A traditional road bike with narrow tires might find some unpaved sections challenging. A gravel or hybrid bike might offer a more comfortable experience on mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, transitioning from vast plains of rainfed cereals to meadows dotted with ancient holm oaks. The route also offers views of the Guadiana River, with its significant ecological value, including wetlands and areas important for diverse flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several interesting spots. You can see the Plaza de San Juan Bautista and Parish Church of San Juan Bautista in Abenójar, the settlement of Saceruela, and the Los Ejidos de Luciana Recreational Area. You'll also encounter sections of the historic Cañada Real Segoviana.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, making them ideal for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be less maintained in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Information specific to dogs on this exact route is not available. However, when cycling in rural areas and natural parks like Campo de Calatrava, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially if encountering livestock or other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this area, particularly in Campo de Calatrava?
faqs.answer: This route traverses the region of Campo de Calatrava. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads or established trails in this area. However, always respect private property and any local regulations, especially within protected natural spaces.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Alcolea de Calatrava - Los Pozuelos de Calatrava, Cañada Real Segoviana, Los Pozuelos de Calatrava - La Viñuela, and the Camino Natural del Guadiana, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the towns of Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and other basic amenities to refuel and rest during your ride.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
faqs.answer: The Guadiana River region is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, especially around wetlands, and other local fauna adapted to the Mediterranean landscape, including small mammals and reptiles. The area's ecological value is significant, with many Special Protection Areas (SPAs).
